heading: Agreements and leases: provision for change
label: 8728 Agreements and leases: provision for change
text: Every unit or cooperative plan of development and operation and every lease affecting lands owned by the United States within Naval Petroleum Reserve Numbered 2 and the oil shale reserves shall contain a provision authorizing the Secretary, subject to approval by the President and to any limitation in the plan or lease, to change from time to time the rate of prospecting and development on, and the quantity and rate of production from, lands of the United States under the plan or lease, notwithstanding any other provision of law.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7428(proviso of 6th par.).34 U.S.C. 524(6th par., proviso, of amended 3d and 4th provisos), ; , ; , .June 4, 1920, ch. 22841 Stat. 813June 30, 1938, ch. 851, § 152 Stat. 1252June 17, 1944, ch. 26258 Stat. 282The words “entered into after ” and “entered into subsequent to ” are omitted as surplusage. The words “in his discretion” are omitted as surplusage.July 1, 1937July 1, 1937

Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 7428 of this title2000— struck out “, except a plan authorized by ,” after “development and operation”.Pub. L. 106–398section 7426 of this title1976— substituted “within Naval Petroleum Reserve Numbered 2 and the oil shale reserves shall contain a provision authorizing the Secretary” for “within the naval petroleum and oil shale reserves shall contain a provision authorizing the Secretary of the Navy”.Pub. L. 94–2581962— included leases affecting lands within oil shale reserves.Pub. L. 87–796

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019

Delegation of FunctionsFunctions of the President under this section were delegated to the Secretary of Energy by Ex. Ord. No. 12929, , 59 F.R. 50473, set out as a note under .Sept. 29, 1994section 7427 of this title
